text: PRNP (prion protein) on chr20p13 encodes the major prion protein. Pathogenic variants in PRNP are associated with Creutzfeldt-Jakob disease, fatal familial insomnia, and Gerstmann-Straussler-Scheinker syndrome. Penetrance varies by variant.
The PRNP E200K variant is the most common cause of genetic prion disease worldwide. ClinGen classifies PRNP as definitively associated with prion disease with autosomal dominant inheritance.

BMP2 (bone morphogenetic protein 2) on chr20p12.3 plays a critical role in skeletal development and bone formation. Heterozygous loss-of-function variants are associated with short stature and facial dysmorphism.

AURKA (aurora kinase A) on chr20q13.2 encodes a mitotic serine/threonine kinase involved in centrosome maturation. Somatic amplification is commonly observed in multiple cancer types but germline variants are typically benign.

TOP1 (DNA topoisomerase I) on chr20q12 encodes an enzyme that relieves torsional stress during DNA replication. No pathogenic germline variants have been established in ClinVar. Somatic mutations may affect chemotherapy response.

GNAS on chr20q13.32 encodes the stimulatory G-protein alpha subunit. Gain-of-function variants cause McCune-Albright syndrome and fibrous dysplasia. Loss-of-function variants cause pseudohypoparathyroidism type Ia (Albright hereditary osteodystrophy).

GNAS demonstrates complex imprinting with tissue-specific expression patterns. ClinGen classifies GNAS as definitively associated with pseudohypoparathyroidism with strong evidence for haploinsufficiency mechanism.

CDH22 (cadherin 22) on chr20q13.1 encodes a calcium-dependent cell adhesion protein expressed primarily in the brain. Variants of uncertain significance have been reported in individuals with neurodevelopmental phenotypes.

MAFB (MAF bZIP transcription factor B) on chr20q12 encodes a transcription factor involved in hindbrain and kidney development. Pathogenic missense variants cause multicentric carpotarsal osteolysis syndrome with autosomal dominant inheritance.

JAG1 (jagged canonical Notch ligand 1) on chr20p12.2 encodes a Notch signaling ligand essential for bile duct and cardiac development. Loss-of-function variants cause Alagille syndrome, characterized by bile duct paucity, cardiac defects, and skeletal anomalies.

ClinGen classifies JAG1 as definitively associated with Alagille syndrome with autosomal dominant inheritance. Over 500 pathogenic variants have been reported, including deletions, truncating, and missense variants.

PLCG1 (phospholipase C gamma 1) on chr20q12 encodes a signaling enzyme involved in T-cell receptor and growth factor signaling. Somatic gain-of-function variants have been identified in angioimmunoblastic T-cell lymphoma.

SRC (proto-oncogene tyrosine-protein kinase) on chr20q11.23 encodes a non-receptor tyrosine kinase involved in cell growth and differentiation. Germline pathogenic variants causing thrombocytopenia have been reported with autosomal dominant inheritance.

ASXL1 (ASXL transcriptional regulator 1) on chr20q11.21 encodes a polycomb group protein involved in transcriptional regulation. Truncating germline variants cause Bohring-Opitz syndrome, a severe developmental disorder.

NTSR1 (neurotensin receptor 1) on chr20q13.33 encodes a G-protein coupled receptor for neurotensin. No established pathogenic germline variants exist in ClinVar; variants reported are classified as benign or likely benign.
